goal: Land the `find_similar` MCP tool — a JSON-RPC handler in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` that accepts a code snippet, embeds it via the injected `EmbeddingSource`, queries the per-branch `code_chunks` LanceDB table opened by `BranchManager` via `Table::query().nearest_to(query_vec).limit(N).execute()`, and returns the top-N semantically similar code chunks ranked by similarity score. Frozen acceptance test selector: `-p ucil-daemon server::test_find_similar_tool` (master-plan §3.2 line 219 + §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1791 — closes Phase 2 Week 8 and the entire Phase 2 envelope).
plan_summary: Introduce a small `FindSimilarExecutor` aggregate (Arc<BranchManager> + Arc<dyn EmbeddingSource> + default branch name) and a `McpServer::with_find_similar_executor(...)` builder mirroring the `with_g2_sources` shape (WO-0048 / WO-0063 precedent). Add a `handle_find_similar` async method that parses `arguments.snippet` + `arguments.max_results` + `arguments.branch`, embeds the snippet via the injected source, opens the per-branch `code_chunks` LanceDB table via `BranchManager::branch_vectors_dir(branch)` → `lancedb::connect(uri).execute().await` → `conn.open_table("code_chunks").execute().await` (the same pattern `LancedbChunkIndexer::index_paths` uses at `lancedb_indexer.rs:660-672`), runs `Table::query().nearest_to(query_vec).limit(N).execute()`, drains the resulting `RecordBatchStream`, and projects the 12 columns into JSON `_meta.hits[]` ranked by similarity. Wire the dispatch in `handle_line` mirroring lines 580-584. Promote `crate::executor::TestEmbeddingSource` from `struct` to `pub(crate)` (or move to `crate::test_support`) so `pub async fn test_find_similar_tool()` in `server.rs` can build a fixture via the WO-0064 `LancedbChunkIndexer` + `TestEmbeddingSource` pattern. Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F08.sh` that runs the frozen-selector test plus a literal-grep guard for rename drift.
scope_in: 1. Add NEW pub-struct `FindSimilarExecutor` (in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` OR a NEW module `crates/ucil-daemon/src/find_similar.rs` re-exported from `lib.rs` — executor's choice; module-coherence per DEC-0005 favours a new module if `find_similar.rs` exceeds ~150 LOC). Constructor: `pub fn new(branch_manager: Arc<BranchManager>, embedding_source: Arc<dyn EmbeddingSource>, default_branch: impl Into<String>) -> Self`. Fields: `branch_manager`, `embedding_source`, `default_branch: String`. Optional accessors: `pub fn default_branch(&self) -> &str`. Document the per-branch LanceDB connection contract inline + cite master-plan §12.2 lines 1321-1346 + DEC-0008.

2. Add field `find_similar: Option<Arc<FindSimilarExecutor>>` on the `McpServer` struct (`server.rs:349`). Initialise to `None` in `McpServer::new()` AND in `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph()` so phase-1 invariant #9 stays preserved when the executor is not attached.

3. Add builder `pub fn with_find_similar_executor(mut self, executor: Arc<FindSimilarExecutor>) -> Self` on `McpServer` (mirror `with_g2_sources` at `server.rs:441`). Document chain-with-`with_knowledge_graph` per the existing builder rustdoc pattern. `#[must_use]` per the existing builder.

4. Add async fn `handle_find_similar(id: &Value, params: &Value, executor: &FindSimilarExecutor) -> Value` (mirror `handle_search_code` at `server.rs:814` — same `id`/`params` plumbing, returns the MCP envelope). Acquire a per-call `tokio::time::timeout(QUERY_TIMEOUT, ...)` wrapper around the embedding + LanceDB calls (named const, e.g. `FIND_SIMILAR_QUERY_TIMEOUT` of 5-10 s). Per language-agnostic invariant 'All async code uses tokio::time::timeout on any await that touches IO'.

5. `handle_find_similar` argument parsing: (a) `arguments.snippet` REQUIRED string — missing/non-string → JSON-RPC `-32602` invalid params (mirror `find_definition`'s `arguments.name` shape at `server.rs:653`); (b) `arguments.max_results` OPTIONAL u64, default 10, clamp to `[1, 100]` (cite a frozen const `DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS = 10` and `MAX_RESULTS_CAP = 100` inline; document why); (c) `arguments.branch` OPTIONAL string, default `executor.default_branch()`.

6. `handle_find_similar` execution: (a) call `executor.embedding_source.embed(snippet).await`, surface `EmbeddingSourceError` as `result.isError = true` with `_meta.error_kind = "embedding_failed"` + the error message (NOT a JSON-RPC error envelope — master-plan §3.2 UX contract favours user-facing errors as `isError: true` for runtime failures, JSON-RPC `error` only for protocol/params violations); (b) validate `query_vec.len() == executor.embedding_source.dim()`, else `result.isError = true` with `_meta.error_kind = "dim_mismatch"`; (c) compute `branch_vectors_dir = executor.branch_manager.branch_vectors_dir(branch)`; (d) `lancedb::connect(uri).execute().await` — if the dir doesn't exist or the connection fails, surface as `result.isError = true` with `_meta.error_kind = "branch_not_found"`; (e) `conn.open_table("code_chunks").execute().await` — if the table doesn't exist (e.g. brand-new branch, never indexed), surface as `result.isError = true` with `_meta.error_kind = "table_not_found"`; (f) run `table.query().nearest_to(query_vec.as_slice()).map_err(...)?.limit(max_results).execute().await` and drain the resulting `RecordBatchStream` to a `Vec<RecordBatch>` via `futures::TryStreamExt::try_collect`.

7. `handle_find_similar` result projection: for each row in each `RecordBatch`, extract the 12 columns (`id`, `file_path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `content`, `language`, `symbol_name`, `symbol_kind`, `embedding`, `token_count`, `file_hash`, `indexed_at`) plus the synthetic `_distance` column LanceDB appends to `nearest_to` results. Project to `Hit { file_path: String, start_line: i32, end_line: i32, content: String, language: String, symbol_name: Option<String>, symbol_kind: Option<String>, similarity_score: f64 }` where `similarity_score = 1.0 / (1.0 + f64::from(_distance))` (monotonic descending in `_distance`; document the formula inline). Skip the raw `embedding` + `token_count` + `file_hash` + `indexed_at` columns from the projection — they are storage details the consumer doesn't need.

8. `handle_find_similar` MCP envelope: `_meta = { tool: "find_similar", source: "lancedb+coderankembed", branch: <resolved>, query_dim: <usize>, hits_count: <usize>, hits: [<Hit>, ...] }`. Sort `hits` by `similarity_score` DESCENDING before serialising (top hit first). Set `result.isError = false`. `result.content` array carries one `text` block summarising `hits_count` matches.

10. Promote `TestEmbeddingSource` visibility for cross-module reuse: in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs:2700`, change `struct TestEmbeddingSource` to `pub(crate) struct TestEmbeddingSource` AND `pub(crate) fn build_synthetic_chunker_for_lancedb_f04()` AND `pub(crate) async fn read_table_rows_for_lancedb_f04(...)` AND `pub(crate) const SYNTHETIC_TOKENIZER_JSON_FOR_LANCEDB_F04` (or whichever helpers the new test imports). Document the visibility promotion in the commit body — pattern: 'WO-0066 second consumer of the WO-0064 test fixtures; promote `pub(crate)` per WO-0064 deferred carve-out'. Alternative if the executor prefers tighter scope: move ALL four helpers to `crate::test_support` (already exists per `ls crates/ucil-daemon/src/`) gated on `#[cfg(test)]` — executor's choice.

11. NEW test: `pub async fn test_find_similar_tool()` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (frozen selector — `cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_similar_tool` MUST resolve). Use `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]` to mirror `executor::test_lancedb_incremental_indexing`. Inline rustdoc lists each sub-assertion (SA1..SA8) so the verifier can map failures back to specific assertion numbers per the WO-0064 / WO-0048 verification-report convention.

12. Test fixture: (a) `tempfile::tempdir()` for the repo root; (b) `branches_root = repo.path().join(".ucil/branches")` + `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&branches_root).await`; (c) `BranchManager::new(&branches_root)`, then `mgr.create_branch_table("main", None).await`; (d) build `chunker = Arc::new(tokio::sync::Mutex::new(build_synthetic_chunker_for_lancedb_f04()))` per WO-0064 line 2825; (e) `source = Arc::new(TestEmbeddingSource { dim: 768 })`; (f) write 3-5 small Rust source files (e.g. `src/foo.rs`, `src/bar.rs`, `src/baz.rs`) with DISTINCT multi-statement bodies so the chunker emits ≥1 chunk per file; (g) construct `LancedbChunkIndexer::new(mgr.clone(), "main", chunker, source.clone())` and call `indexer.index_paths(repo.path(), &[foo, bar, baz, ...]).await`; (h) build `executor = Arc::new(FindSimilarExecutor::new(mgr.clone(), source.clone(), "main"))`; (i) `let kg = Arc::new(Mutex::new(KnowledgeGraph::open_in_memory()?))` (or `with_knowledge_graph` skipped if `McpServer::new()` is sufficient); (j) `let server = McpServer::new().with_find_similar_executor(executor)` (CHAIN with whichever KG path is needed).

13. Test SA1 — happy path envelope: send `tools/call` request `{name: "find_similar", arguments: {snippet: <text>, max_results: 3}}` with `id = 401`. Assert (a) `response.jsonrpc == "2.0"`, (b) `response.id == 401`, (c) `response.error` absent, (d) `response.result.isError == false`. Mirror `test_search_code_fused` SA1 at `server.rs:3236-3255`.

14. Test SA2 — `_meta` shape: assert (a) `_meta.tool == "find_similar"`, (b) `_meta.source` is non-empty string, (c) `_meta.branch == "main"`, (d) `_meta.query_dim == 768`, (e) `_meta.hits_count` is JSON number, (f) `_meta.hits` is JSON array.

15. Test SA3 — hits count and structure: assert `_meta.hits.len() == 3` (or whatever max_results was). Assert each hit carries the projection fields: `file_path` (str non-empty), `start_line` (int >= 1), `end_line` (int >= start_line), `content` (str), `language` (str e.g. "rust"), `symbol_name` (str OR null), `symbol_kind` (str OR null), `similarity_score` (number).

16. Test SA4 — query-vs-corpus IDENTITY match: build the query snippet to be byte-identical to the textual content of one specific corpus chunk (the chunker emits chunk content unchanged from the source slice, so an identical input produces an identical content string AND therefore — under TestEmbeddingSource's deterministic Sha256-derived vectors — an identical 768-d embedding, hence distance ≈ 0 to that chunk). Assert top hit's `file_path` ends with the file the identical chunk came from. Document the pattern inline (this is the key correctness signal for the WO).
17. Test SA5 — similarity ordering: assert `hits[0].similarity_score >= hits[1].similarity_score >= hits[2].similarity_score` (monotonically decreasing per AC09).
18. Test SA6 — error path missing snippet: send `tools/call` `{name: "find_similar", arguments: {}}`. Assert response carries JSON-RPC `error.code == -32602` AND `error.message` mentions `snippet`.

19. Test SA7 — error path unknown branch: send `tools/call` `{name: "find_similar", arguments: {snippet: <text>, branch: "nonexistent-branch"}}`. Assert `response.result.isError == true` AND `_meta.error_kind == "branch_not_found"` (or `table_not_found` — both are acceptable for a nonexistent branch dir; pick one and document why).

20. Test SA8 — fall-through path when no executor attached: build `let server = McpServer::new()` (no `with_find_similar_executor`). Send the same happy-path request. Assert `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true` (the phase-1 stub fall-through at `server.rs:595-613`). This guards against a regression where the new dispatch accidentally runs when the executor isn't attached.

21. NEW script `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F08.sh` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, `IFS=$'\n\t'`). Top-of-file rustdoc cites master-plan §3.2 line 219 + §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1791. Behaviour: (a) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`, (b) frozen-selector grep — `grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*pub async fn test_find_similar_tool\(\)' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (guards against rename drift), (c) frozen-handler grep — `grep -qE 'fn handle_find_similar' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, (d) frozen-builder grep — `grep -qE 'fn with_find_similar_executor' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, (e) `cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_similar_tool --no-fail-fast` (or fall back to `cargo test -p ucil-daemon -- --exact server::test_find_similar_tool` if `cargo nextest` not on PATH), (f) print `[OK] P2-W8-F08 find_similar tool wired and verified` on green, exit 0; print `[FAIL] P2-W8-F08: <reason>` to stderr + exit 1 on failure.

22. Pre-baked mutation contract documented in WO `acceptance` block AND executor's `ready-for-review.md` Mutation-execution-log section. Three mutations, each delegated to the verifier per WO-0061 line 690 (DO NOT commit-then-revert in-line):

- M1 — query-vec replacement: in `handle_find_similar`, replace `nearest_to(query_vec.as_slice())` with `nearest_to(&[0.0f32; 768])` (drops the snippet's contribution). Expected: SA4 fails — top hit no longer matches the identical-text chunk because the all-zeros query vector finds chunks whose Sha256-derived components are closest to 0, which won't be the identical-text file. Verifier restoration: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (or wherever the handler lives).

- M2 — embedding bypass: replace `executor.embedding_source.embed(snippet).await` call with `Ok::<Vec<f32>, _>(vec![0.0f32; executor.embedding_source.dim()])`. Expected: same as M1 — SA4 fails because the bypassed embedding loses the snippet's content signal. Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`.

- M3 — sort-order omission: comment out the `_meta.hits.sort_by(...)` call (or equivalent — wherever similarity_score-descending ordering is enforced). Expected: SA5 fails on the LanceDB stream-order leak (LanceDB's `nearest_to` results are typically already distance-ordered, but this is not contractually guaranteed across versions; defence-in-depth sort is load-bearing). Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`.

23. Pre-flight word-ban (per WO-0061 line 686): NO literal `mock`, `fake`, `stub` substrings (case-insensitive) in PRODUCTION code paths — i.e. in any new lines added to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` or `crates/ucil-daemon/src/find_similar.rs` (if the executor chooses the new-module path) OUTSIDE the `#[cfg(test)]` block. Test code is exempt per WO-0048 line 363 (canonical test trait-impl naming). Pre-commit grep: split src files between cfg(test) and non-cfg(test) regions and assert the non-cfg(test) region is mock/fake/stub-free.

All required deps — `lancedb`, `arrow-array`, `arrow-schema`, `tokio`, `async-trait`, `tempfile` (dev), `futures` (dev), `tokenizers` (dev), `sha2`, `serde_json`, `tracing`, `ucil-embeddings` — are already present per the existing `Cargo.toml` (lines 26-92). Verified by the executor BEFORE writing handler code (run `cargo check -p ucil-daemon` against the proposed handler shape; if a missing dep surfaces, the executor SHOULD escalate via a proposed-decision rather than silently adding a dep).

Commit-cadence soft target: 6 commits — `refactor(daemon):` (TestEmbeddingSource + helpers visibility promotion in `executor.rs`), `feat(daemon):` (FindSimilarExecutor struct + module rustdoc), `feat(daemon):` (handle_find_similar + McpServer field/builder), `feat(daemon):` (handle_line dispatch wiring), `test(daemon):` (test_find_similar_tool + 8 sub-assertions), `chore(scripts):` (verify/P2-W8-F08.sh), `docs(work-orders):` (0066-ready-for-review.md). Per-commit LOC ≤ 50 soft target per CLAUDE.md root commit cadence rules; cite DEC-0005 + WO-0064 precedent in the commit body if the test-file commit exceeds 200 LOC (the test fixture + 8 sub-assertions WILL exceed 200 LOC).

26. Rustdoc discipline: `handle_find_similar`, `FindSimilarExecutor`, and `McpServer::with_find_similar_executor` MUST carry rustdoc citing master-plan §3.2 line 219 + §18 line 1791. Inline citations of WO-0064 (LancedbChunkIndexer pattern) + DEC-0008 (UCIL-internal-trait-boundaries) on the relevant types. Per-crate `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` REMAINS green.

27. Tracing discipline: emit `tracing::info_span!("ucil.daemon.find_similar", branch = %branch, max_results = max_results, query_dim = query_vec.len())` around the handler body per master-plan §15.2 span-naming convention. `tracing::warn!(...)` on error paths with the structured `error_kind` field.

28. Coverage gate posture: `crates/ucil-daemon` coverage MUST NOT regress below pre-WO baseline (run `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json` before AND after; informational floor `>= 80.0` per the WO-0064 / WO-0065 standing protocol). The new handler + builder SHOULD ADD coverage given the test exercises them.
29. Upstream-API research checklist for `lancedb 0.16` query path (per WO-0060 line 644 + WO-0065 line 770):
- (a) Does `Table::query()` return a builder? → yes, `lancedb::query::Query`.
- (b) `nearest_to(slice)` signature? → returns `lancedb::Result<VectorQuery>`; `.unwrap()`/`?` per the bench precedent at `crates/ucil-embeddings/benches/vector_query.rs:302`.
- (c) `.limit(N)` + `.execute()` → `.execute().await?` returns `RecordBatchStream`. Drain via `futures::TryStreamExt::try_collect` (already used in `executor.rs:2750+`).

- (d) `_distance` column shape on the result `RecordBatch`? → `Float32` per LanceDB convention. Project to `f32` via `arrow_array::Float32Array::value(i)`. Confirm with the WO-0064 helper `read_table_rows_for_lancedb_f04` at `executor.rs:2746` to see the same `arrow_array::cast::AsArray` projection pattern.

- (e) Per-call timeout: `tokio::time::timeout(FIND_SIMILAR_QUERY_TIMEOUT, async { ... }).await` — pick a const value (suggested `5000 ms` for the embedding + LanceDB nearest_to combined). Document inline.

30. PROTOC toolchain pre-flight (carry from WO-0058 line 553 / WO-0064 / WO-0065): `lancedb 0.16` brings `prost-build` via `lance-encoding` / `lance-file` build scripts → `protoc` MUST be on PATH. Standing pattern: protobuf v25.1 release zip → `~/.local/bin/protoc` + `~/.local/include/google/protobuf/*.proto`, then `export PROTOC=~/.local/bin/protoc PROTOC_INCLUDE=~/.local/include` for every `cargo` invocation. Disclose in RFR §"Disclosed deviations" verbatim — verifier needs the same toolchain.

31. Phase-1 gate regression check: `bash scripts/gate/phase-1.sh` MUST exit 0 post-merge (carry from WO-0058..WO-0065 standing protocol). The new MCP tool wiring touches the dispatch table indirectly (no change to the 22-tool registry) so phase-1 invariant #9 stays preserved for the un-attached path.

32. Out-of-scope effectiveness re-runs: phase-1 effectiveness-evaluator child agents are EXPECTED to commit `ucil-build/verification-reports/effectiveness-phase-1.md` updates on the WO-0066 feat branch (carry from WO-0061 line 779 + WO-0065 line 779 anti-pattern observation). Critic / verifier may flag as a Warning but accept as benign gate-side artefact. Do NOT add `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` to `forbidden_paths` — would block legitimate gate-side commits.
scope_out: Daemon startup wiring of `FindSimilarExecutor` into `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` / `lifecycle.rs` / `startup.rs` — F08 is a HANDLER-LEVEL WO; production wiring (CodeRankEmbeddingSource::load(model_dir) + BranchManager::new + McpServer::with_find_similar_executor in the daemon's startup orchestrator) is deferred to a follow-up WO that bundles startup wiring for find_similar AND for the indexer's production background mode AND for the search_code G2 LanceDB hit-source. The handler is callable from any test or future-startup path that injects an executor.

Production ANN-index creation (`Table::create_index(IvfHnswPq)`) on the production-side `code_chunks` table — F07 (WO-0065) documents the index-creation pattern in the synthetic bench; production-side IVF/HNSW index creation hooks into `LancedbChunkIndexer::index_paths` post-batch and is deferred to a follow-up daemon-side WO. The find_similar test's small corpus does NOT need an ANN index — `Table::query().nearest_to(...)` performs a flat scan when no index exists, which is correct for small corpora and exercises the same `RecordBatchStream` shape the production path will emit.

Real `CodeRankEmbed` embeddings in tests — `TestEmbeddingSource` is the canonical UCIL-internal-trait-impl pattern per DEC-0008 §4 + WO-0064 line 270 (the existing `TestEmbeddingSource` impl in `executor.rs:2700-2726`). Production code uses `CodeRankEmbeddingSource` from `lancedb_indexer.rs:326+`. F08 does NOT require deploying CodeRankEmbed weights to verifier hosts.

Recall@10 benchmark (master-plan §18 line 1789's third leg) — out of scope. Recall@10 needs a labelled query/answer corpus and is NOT a feature in the current `feature-list.json` Phase 2 envelope (F08 is the last open Phase-2 feature). Defer to a Phase-3+ benchmarking WO if the spec ever calls it out.
Multi-branch cross-search — F08 only queries one branch per request via `arguments.branch`. Aggregating results across multiple branches is deferred to a future fusion WO.

G2-fused merging of `find_similar` results with G1/G2 search — F08 returns LanceDB-only hits. Cross-tool fusion (Probe + ripgrep + LanceDB merging) lives in `search_code` (P2-W7-F06, already passing per WO-0063). A future WO may bundle find_similar's hits into the G2 fused path; deferred.
Production wiring of 4 `G1Source` impls + tracing on `execute_g1` / `fuse_g1` — deferred to P2-W7-F05 per WO-0047/48/51/55/56/58/59/60/61/62/65 standing scope_out.

Resolving the `ndarray 0.16` workspace dep vs `ort`-internal `ndarray 0.17` duplication — carry from WO-0058 / WO-0059 / WO-0060 / WO-0061 / WO-0062 / WO-0065. F08 does not write embedding vectors via `ndarray` (the handler reads existing `embedding` column from LanceDB and the projection drops it). Defer to a future ndarray-version-bump WO.

Coverage workaround — same `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` + per-crate clean standing protocol. Carry from WO-0058..WO-0065 (now 21st consecutive WO under the same workaround). Follow-up trigger remains resolution of escalation `20260419-0152-monitor-phase1-gate-red-integration-gaps.md` PLUS a `coverage-gate.sh` harness improvement.
`rand` workspace-routing cleanup (carry from WO-0065 line 770) — F08 does NOT consume `rand`. Defer.
`cargo doc -p ucil-embeddings --no-deps` 7 broken intra-doc links (carry from WO-0062 line 741 / WO-0065 line 786) — F08 does not touch `ucil-embeddings`. Defer.

Effectiveness-gate `rs-line` fixture flake — open escalation `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`. Orthogonal to F08. Defer per the existing escalation's three-options Phase-8 audit note.
Updating `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` codification of any F08-emergent patterns — orthogonal docs-WO.
acceptance: AC01 — `pub async fn test_find_similar_tool()` exists in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` and is decorated with `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]`. Frozen selector: `cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_find_similar_tool` (and `cargo test -p ucil-daemon -- --exact server::test_find_similar_tool`) MUST resolve.

AC02 — `McpServer` struct (`server.rs:349`) gains a `find_similar: Option<Arc<FindSimilarExecutor>>` field. `McpServer::new()` initialises it to `None`. `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph()` initialises it to `None`.
AC03 — Builder method `pub fn with_find_similar_executor(self, executor: Arc<FindSimilarExecutor>) -> Self` exists on `McpServer` and is `#[must_use]`. Mirrors `with_g2_sources` at `server.rs:441`.

AC04 — `FindSimilarExecutor` struct exists (in `server.rs` or `find_similar.rs`) with `pub fn new(branch_manager: Arc<BranchManager>, embedding_source: Arc<dyn EmbeddingSource>, default_branch: impl Into<String>) -> Self`.

AC05 — `handle_line` dispatch in `server.rs` routes `tools/call name == "find_similar"` to `handle_find_similar` when `self.find_similar.is_some()`; falls through to the phase-1 stub at line 595-613 otherwise (preserves invariant #9 for the un-attached case).

AC06 — `handle_find_similar` parses `arguments.snippet` (REQUIRED string, missing/non-string → JSON-RPC `-32602`), `arguments.max_results` (OPTIONAL u64, default 10, clamped `[1, 100]`), `arguments.branch` (OPTIONAL string, default executor's `default_branch`).
AC07 — Embedding failure (`EmbeddingSourceError`) → `result.isError = true`, `_meta.error_kind == "embedding_failed"`, `_meta.error_message` non-empty.
AC08 — Dim mismatch (`query_vec.len() != embedding_source.dim()`) → `result.isError = true`, `_meta.error_kind == "dim_mismatch"`.
AC09 — Unknown branch (vectors dir absent OR LanceDB connect fails) → `result.isError = true`, `_meta.error_kind == "branch_not_found"`.
AC10 — Missing `code_chunks` table (table never created on the branch) → `result.isError = true`, `_meta.error_kind == "table_not_found"`.

AC11 — Happy path returns `result.isError == false`, `_meta.tool == "find_similar"`, `_meta.source` (non-empty string, e.g. `"lancedb+coderankembed"` OR similar — executor's choice but documented inline), `_meta.branch == <resolved>`, `_meta.query_dim == 768` (TestEmbeddingSource default), `_meta.hits_count` matches `_meta.hits.len()`.

AC12 — Each hit in `_meta.hits` carries the projection fields: `file_path` (str), `start_line` (int >= 1), `end_line` (int >= start_line), `content` (str), `language` (str), `symbol_name` (str OR null), `symbol_kind` (str OR null), `similarity_score` (number).
AC13 — `_meta.hits` are sorted by `similarity_score` DESCENDING. For all i in `0..hits.len()-1`, `hits[i].similarity_score >= hits[i+1].similarity_score`.
